title: Chemistry
description: A collection of RAND research on the topic of Chemistry
text:
Robust Biosecurity Measures Should Be Standardized at Scientific Cloud Labs
Cloud labs are revolutionizing scientific research with remote, automated environments, offering on-demand scientific services. With robust security measures, they can maximize potential while ensuring safe and secure research environments.
Gaps in Toxic Industrial Chemical Model Systems: Improvements and Changes Over Past 10 Years
To assess the hazards of the releases of toxic chemicals to the atmosphere, comprehensive model systems are often used. This paper reviews knowledge gaps that were resolved in the past ten years by new theoretical and experimental research.
Ammonia – Why the Big Stink?
Ammonia pollution harms human health and reduces the richness and diversity of the environment. As new policy frameworks are implemented in the UK, there is an opportunity to support farmers to make the changes necessary to reduce ammonia pollution.
Zenith-sky Observations of Stratospheric Gases: The Sensitivity of Air Mass Factors to Geophysical Parameters and the Influence of Tropospheric Clouds
The retrieval of accurate vertical column amounts of stratospheric constituents from zenith-sky spectroscopy is dependent on accurately modeling the transfer of radiation through the atmosphere and calculating suitable air mass factors (AMFs).
Separation of Hydrohalocarbons and Chlorofluorocarbons Using a Cyclodextrin Gas Solid Chromatography Capillary Column
Chromatographic analysis of very volatile hydrohalocarbons and chlorofluorocarbons has proved to be a difficult task due to the generally poor retention of these compounds on commercially available wall coated open tubular capillary columns.
Chemical Equilibrium Problems with Unbounded Constraint Sets.
An investigation of the use of mathematical models to explore the chemical aspects of physiological systems; this deals with the theoretical and computational aspects of understanding the chemistry of human physiological function. The question of ex...
Ion Chemistry Governing Mesospheric Electron Densities.
An investigation of the physicochemical processes governing electron density in the earth's mesosphere. Ionization results from the weak background cosmic radiation, daytime solar X rays, and special events, and is ordinarily detected by its effect ...
Lower Bounds to the Critical Rayleigh Number in Completely Confined Regions
Minimum Rayleigh number that will induce a state of convective motion in a Boussinesq fluid where temperature gradient is in direction of body force.
Protein Binding of Small Ions--A Mathematical Model of Serum Albumin.
Examines the role of serum albumin in blood chemistry with respect to the binding of hydrogen, chloride, and calcium. Serum albumin is considered as a mathematical entity. Emphasis is on the interpretation of binding data using the Linderstrom-Lang...
Acid-Base Metabolism and the Proton Condition.
Application of the proton condition of inorganic chemistry to the problem of describing the net acid content in a biological system. A "proton content" is defined that expresses the net acid content in terms of the detailed chemical composition of t...
